summaries.95985: Comments confirm the bug on iOS and Android; users share workarounds involving Overlay + keyboard-visibility plugin to display a floating "Done" button on iOS. No official fix or PR is mentioned; the issue remains open and stalled with the workaround as the only practical approach.
summaries.32583: One commenter provides a working code-level workaround using `Scaffold(resizeToAvoidBottomInset: !showEmojiKeyboard)` toggled when switching between the emoji panel and the system keyboard, preserving TextField position without layout jitter. The pub package `chat_bottom_container` is also suggested. No official PR; status is stalled.
summaries.155305: Triager reproduced the issue on stable 3.24.3 and master 3.26 (confirmed affected platforms not explicitly listed but implied all). Issue was CC'd to @Renzo-Olivares for triage. No fix or PR mentioned; status is open with P2.
summaries.164539: Comments confirm the bug is Windows-specific (not reproducible on macOS or standard web). The reporter confirms it happens on both Windows desktop and Windows web (Chrome/Edge). A triager notes the `triaged-text-input` label cleanup. No fix or PR mentioned; status is awaiting further investigation.
summaries.181759: Comments identify the root cause: on macOS and Linux, dynamically changing `textDirection` during IME composition triggers a `wstring_convert::to_bytes` exception in the engine (built with `-fno-exceptions`), causing an abort; on Android the IME composing state is invalidated, producing `?`/replacement characters. iOS and web cannot reproduce. A crash log from macOS shows the engine decoding an invalid JSON surrogate pair. A comment in #183112 indicates this cannot be fixed in the framework and requires fixes in each affected embedder (macOS, Linux). Status: needs engine/embedder fix.
summaries.146973: Comments narrow this to a Xiaomi device (Redmi K40) with its proprietary secure keyboard; multiple Pixel, Realme, and Galaxy Tab devices could not reproduce. Root cause appears to be a conflict between Xiaomi's secure keyboard switching and Flutter's window resize behavior when toggling `obscureText`. No fix or PR; labeled device-specific and stalled.
summaries.52863: A commenter identifies the root cause: when the TextField is not centered in its accessibility semantics node, iOS double-tap from VoiceOver does not properly bring up the keyboard. A workaround is to wrap the TextField in `Semantics(container: true)` to tighten the semantics node. Issue remains open; fix requires changes in the iOS accessibility bridge.
summaries.83089: Triagers reproduced two sub-issues: selection handles moving far above the field when "Select All" is used on long text, and handles persisting when text is scrolled outside the field. Confirmed on stable 2.2.0 / master 2.3. No fix or PR; open and stalled.
summaries.98265: Comments confirm the cursor-drag issue with Gboard specifically: reproduced on Flutter 2.10 and 2.11 with Gboard on OnePlus 5T (Android 10) and OnePlus 7 (Android 11) but not with Minuum keyboard, Samsung keyboard, or Samsung devices. Root cause is Gboard-specific IME behavior restarting the input method when the composing region changes. CC'd to @LongCatIsLooong. No fix; stalled.
summaries.160009: Comments identify that the crash occurs only on physical iPad devices (not simulators) when tapping inside the uncommitted composing region with `stylusHandwritingEnabled: false`. The stack trace points to `[FlutterTextInputView closestPositionToPoint:withinRange:]` assuming `_selectionRects` is non-empty, but setting `stylusHandwritingEnabled: false` prevents `_selectionRects` from being populated (traced to `editable_text.dart#L4476`). Confirmed on iPad Air M1 (iOS 18.2). No PR yet; status is waiting for an engine fix.
summaries.182941: Comments confirm the bug is reproducible on physical Android devices with a hardware keyboard connected via USB-C (not on emulators or Mac+Android setups). The issue is that when `TextInputType.none` is set, `Ctrl+V` key events are not received by the framework until at least one character has been typed or the on-screen keyboard shown once. Investigation shows the first `Ctrl+V` is simply not forwarded from the Android embedder; subsequent ones work. Routed to the engine team (Android embedder). Status: awaiting engine investigation.
summaries.93909: Testers could not reproduce with Microsoft SwiftKey on Samsung S10+ but the reporter confirmed it on SwiftKey on a Huawei Nova 5T and reportedly on Huawei P8 and Samsung A72. The reporter later states the device was no longer available and they could not reproduce on another phone. Status: closed in practice due to inability to reproduce; labeled device-specific.
summaries.84445: Comments confirm the bug persists on stable and master through Flutter 3.22. A user found that setting `autocorrect: false` alongside `obscureText: true` and `AutofillHints.newPassword` fixes the autofill hint on some versions, but others report this workaround does not work on iOS 16.2+ or Flutter 3.3+/3.7/3.22. Status: stalled, no fix or PR.
summaries.171068: Comments confirm the bug on iOS 18.5 with the system Simplified Pinyin keyboard; invisible U+2006 separator characters remain in the field text after focus leaves during composition. iOS native UITextField cleans these up correctly. Reproduced on stable 3.32.4 and master 3.33. A comment notes the fix will be easier once `EditableTextState` handles `resignFirstResponder` synchronously. Status: open, no PR.
summaries.77023: Comments identify the root cause as CanvasKit dynamically loading CJK fonts when unknown characters are first encountered; switching to the HTML renderer avoids the issue. The workaround of using a custom font family (bundled) also resolves it. Community notes this affects Hebrew and Arabic too, and persists through 3.32.4. The issue was marked P1 but has had no recent owner; status is stalled.
summaries.9383: Comments suggest using `integration_test` package (which supports `tester.testTextInput.receiveAction(TextInputAction.done)`) as a replacement for `flutter_driver` for keyboard event simulation. However, this does not cover physical key presses in integration tests. A commenter notes `tester.sendKeyEvent` in integration tests also does not reliably inject text on device. Status: stalled; the original `flutter_driver` API was never implemented.
summaries.156801: Root cause identified in a comment: `TextInputPlugin.java` uses `TYPE_TEXT_VARIATION_VISIBLE_PASSWORD` when `enableSuggestions: false`, which prevents Gboard's language-switching feature. This also affects `TextInputType.visiblePassword`. Confirmed on Pixel 7 (not device-specific) on stable 3.24.3 and master 3.27. A commenter notes the same issue on iOS with `visiblePassword` and suggests `name` as a fix. No PR yet; status is awaiting fix from the engine/Android team.
summaries.153240: Comments confirm `_hasVisualOverflow` in `RenderEditable` only considers horizontal scroll, so vertical overflow from reduced-height `TextStyle` is never clipped until horizontal overflow occurs. Reproduced on Android and macOS. The fix suggested is to always clip when `clipBehavior != Clip.none` or to always check vertical overflow too. No PR filed; open for over 6 months, stalled.
summaries.172437: A triager confirms reproduction on Android and iOS with stable 3.32.7 and master 3.33. A community comment provides a workaround: wrap the Drawer's ListView in a Padding using `MediaQuery.viewInsetsOf` and manually handle bottom padding. No fix or PR; status is open.
summaries.151097: Comments confirm the composing-underline highlight misalignment on Flutter web across Android Chrome, iOS Safari, and desktop macOS Chrome. Does not occur on Android/macOS native apps. Also occurs with `maxLines` set to a fixed value, causing the suggestion list to render at the wrong position. A comment suggests this may self-resolve with native text inputs on web. No PR; stalled.
summaries.123658: A triager confirmed the bug on stable 3.7.8 and master 3.9. `SelectableText` uses `EditableText` internally, so `maxLines` reserves the full height rather than fitting content, unlike `Text`. Workaround is to use `SelectionArea` instead. Issue kept open as a real bug; likely to be fixed only when `SelectableText` is deprecated. Status: stalled.
summaries.91472: Confirmed reproducible on Android (TalkBack does not announce `errorText` when focus returns to a previously-validated field); the error is visible in the semantics node but not announced on re-focus. Not known if intentional. CC'd to @justinmc. No fix or PR; stalled.
summaries.78054: Comments confirm the regression appeared in Flutter 2.0: HID keyboard input (via AppDelegate `keyCommands`) is only received when a TextField is focused, whereas pre-2.0 it was received regardless. The underlying cause appears to be that Flutter 2.0 changed how UIKeyCommands are handled in the engine. No fix; stalled.
summaries.66050: Comments confirm the `fixBelowBaseline` miscalculation persists on stable 3.3.5 and master 3.5. The workaround noted is that at least one app relied on the bug for visually-acceptable baseline placement. The proper fix requires both removing the spurious offset and also properly positioning the baseline under constraint. No PR; open and stalled.
summaries.153079: Root cause narrowed to the `NotificationListener<ScrollNotification>` + `FocusScope.unfocus()` pattern: when a dialog is popped, the focus is restored to the previously focused TextField. Reproduced on stable 3.24+ through 3.29.3. Related to #152001 and #111777. A workaround is to call `FocusManager.instance.primaryFocus?.unfocus()` in the dialog's dismiss handler. No fix; open.
summaries.80669: Comments confirm the bug also applies to macOS desktop (not just web), as a commenter verified the same double-click word-selection behavior in macOS Stickies. Not verified on Windows/Linux. Related to #64550. No fix or PR; stalled.
summaries.92477: A triager confirms the calculator-key layout on Android tablets and iPads is expected behavior: iOS iPad does not support `numberPad` or `phonePad` types, so `TextInputType.number` shows the extended numeric keyboard with operators. This is considered WAI by the framework; CC'd to @LongCatIsLooong for text input triage.
summaries.99139: Comments confirm this is distinct from mobile behavior: on macOS/desktop, trailing whitespace in a multiline TextField does not trigger line wrap, unlike Sublime Text or other native editors. On iOS/mobile, whitespace also does not wrap, but subsequent characters start on a new line. Selection extending horizontally outside the field when selecting text with trailing whitespace is noted as a separate bug. Attributed to Skia-level text layout behavior. No fix; stalled.
summaries.98342: A commenter reproduced the missing text selection toolbar on long-press in Flutter web on Android Chrome with Gboard, but could get the toolbar to appear on Pixel 6 Pro without Gboard. Reporter confirms Gboard is the distinguishing factor. The triage notes this is related to #94130 (double-handle issue). No fix or PR; stalled.
summaries.178108: Comments confirm the stylus stroke offset occurs only when the Jetpack Compose InProgressStrokesView is embedded via `AndroidView` in Flutter; standalone Compose activity works correctly. Reproduced on master 3.38.0+. Assigned to @gmackall but no status updates; labeled for further investigation. No fix or PR yet.
summaries.126497: Comments confirm reproduction on stable 3.10 and master 3.11 on Android, iOS, web, and macOS. The cursor position is wrong when a `WidgetSpan` is used followed by a newline in a custom `TextEditingController`. An engine team member (Skia side) could not reproduce using a single-paragraph example and requested a simpler test case. CC'd to @LongCatIsLooong. Status: stalled pending simpler repro.
summaries.145743: Comments confirm the autocomplete options list fails to scroll back to the first item when keyboard navigation wraps around. Confirmed on stable 3.19.4 and master 3.21. Related to #91795 (similar focus/scroll issue). No fix or PR; stalled.
summaries.108813: Comments confirm the overflow occurs on desktop (macOS) and web but not on Android or iOS. Workaround: set `visualDensity: VisualDensity.standard` in `ThemeData`; the overflow is caused by density-offset adjustments in `InputDecorator`. Possibly a duplicate of #82709. Unassigned after previous assignee did not make progress; open for contribution.
summaries.116836: Comments confirm Android keyboard animation jitter on real devices in release mode; root cause is a vsync timing mismatch where `setViewportMetrics` is called before the framework's vsync process callback. The analogous iOS fix used `engine/pull/37604`. An Android-side contributor notes no clear equivalent Android engine hook was found. #168768 was resolved by #176418 but it is unclear if that covers this specific timing issue. Community continues to report the issue through 2025; labeled duplicate of #168768 but kept open pending confirmation. Status: stalled.
summaries.180865: Comments clarify that the main-thread block on `[UIPasteboard string]` in `getClipboardData` is the reported path, but since latest Flutter uses the system context menu by default this codepath may no longer be hit for paste. A comment notes `FlutterTextInputPlugin.mm:1339` has a similar blocking `hasStrings` call for text input paste, which iOS handles with a progress indicator for slow network clipboard syncs. Reporter confirms no Sentry events after upgrading Flutter, suggesting the system context menu resolved it. A comment notes `SelectableText`/`SelectionArea` may still be affected since they never call `getClipboardData` for paste (so that path is moot for them). Status: effectively resolved for most cases via system context menu; lower-priority to fix the old path.
summaries.171369: A commenter identifies the root cause as the migration of `Autocomplete` to `OverlayPortal` in Flutter 3.22.0 (commit `b6e758a`); the `TextInputAction.next` focus traversal breaks when the Autocomplete is inside a `Column` but works in a `Row`. Verified on stable 3.32.5 and master 3.33. No PR yet; status is open.
summaries.103661: A commenter (likely @Renzo-Olivares) acknowledges the request, agrees delta support for cut/paste actions is desirable, and links to related #100260 and a PR #104198 exploring a solution. Status: acknowledged, pending further work on delta text input.
summaries.120360: A commenter provides a workaround: extend `Border` and override `dimensions` to return the desired padding; extending `BoxDecoration.padding` does not work because it delegates to `border?.dimensions`. No official fix; labeled P3 feature request; stalled.
summaries.179077: Comments confirm the issue persists on Flutter master (3.39.0-pre-301). The bug is specific to Samsung Galaxy Watch running One UI 8 (not reproducible on simulators or older One UI versions). Samsung's default keyboard toggles text monitoring off after initial input, stopping the IME from sending updates to the Flutter text field. Gboard on the same watch works correctly. Labeled as device/OS-version-specific; no fix or PR; stalled.
